title: qPCR Analysis
purpose: Analyze QuantStudio experiments — review amplification curves, assign Cq values, compute ΔΔCt, and export structured results.
learningTime: Learn this tool in under 3 minutes
capabilities: Standard curve and melt curve views

tour.title: Import run data
tour.body: Upload a QuantStudio .eds file or compatible Excel export to begin analysis.

tour.title: Workspace dashboard
tour.body: Review the plate map, amplification traces, and run summary in one unified view.

tour.title: Advanced views
tour.body: Open raw data, averaged replicates, ΔΔCt, results tables, and method details.

tour.title: Export
tour.body: Download a structured Excel workbook with Ct values, ΔΔCt, and run metadata.

overview.purpose: Parse and analyze real-time PCR instrument files for Cq calling, replicate averaging, and relative gene expression.
overview.useCase: Compare target gene expression across samples normalized to a reference gene and calibrator sample.
overview.inputs: QuantStudio .eds files or Excel exports with well-level amplification data.
overview.outputs: Ct tables, ΔΔCt results, charts, and multi-sheet Excel reports.

workflow.title: Upload file
workflow.description: Load an .eds or Excel file from your instrument export.

workflow.title: Review plate
workflow.description: Confirm sample and target assignments on the interactive plate map.

workflow.title: Check amplification
workflow.description: Inspect curves and threshold; omit failed wells if needed.

workflow.title: ΔΔCt analysis
workflow.description: Set reference gene and control sample in the ΔΔCt panel.

workflow.title: Export
workflow.description: Export Excel for records or downstream statistics.

tips: Verify plate layout matches your physical plate before interpreting ΔΔCt.
Omit wells with abnormal amplification or N/A Ct values.
Use biological replicates — technical replicates are averaged automatically where applicable.
Document reference gene and calibrator choices in your lab notebook.

faq.a: QuantStudio .eds files and Excel exports compatible with the parser. If import fails, export from QuantStudio as .eds.
The tool applies the standard ΔΔCt method using your selected reference gene and control sample.
Yes. Omit wells from the results view to exclude them from downstream calculations.
